# Aether

> Aether is an open-source, local observability TUI for Claude Code and Codex.

## Guides

- [Claude Code observability](https://aether.haciensus.com/claude-code-observability/)
- [Codex observability](https://aether.haciensus.com/codex-observability/)
- [AI agent context monitoring](https://aether.haciensus.com/ai-agent-context-monitoring/)

Aether reads the native session files already created by supported coding agents and turns them into a live terminal dashboard. It does not require an SDK, provider API key, hook installation, additional model call, or hosted telemetry service.

## Product

- Website: https://aether.haciensus.com
- Source: https://github.com/connectchiragg/aether
- Releases: https://github.com/connectchiragg/aether/releases/latest
- License: MIT
- Platforms: macOS and Linux

## Capabilities

- Synchronized context, duration, estimated cost, token, turn complexity, and code-diff graphs
- Per-request input attribution across context, tools, MCPs, agents, hooks, memory, documents, compactions, and provider runtime
- Claude Code and Codex session discovery without configuration
- Project-grouped session titles and nested agent traces
- Local-only processing of provider-native JSONL files

## Install

Homebrew users install `connectchiragg/tap/aether`, then run `aether watch`.
